req.title: Patient portal login
req.statement: A patient shall log in using SSO; failed attempts shall be rate-limited and notify the patient.

doc.reasoning: The requirement mandates SSO-based patient login with session establishment on success, rate-limiting (HTTP 429) on excessive failed attempts, and patient notification on failure. Four tests are designed: (1) EP positive – valid SSO assertion yields an authenticated session; (2) NEG negative – invalid SSO assertion is rejected without a session; (3) BVA boundary – exactly N failed attempts before the rate-limit threshold succeeds, while N+1 triggers 429; (4) STC state-transition – system moves correctly through Unauthenticated → Authenticating → Authenticated states. Each test is self-contained via fixtures and resets state before execution.

tests.name: test_valid_sso_login_establishes_session
preconditions: Patient account exists with valid SSO binding
No prior rate-limit state for the test patient
actions: POST /portal/login with a valid signed SSO assertion for test patient
Capture response status code and Set-Cookie header
expected: HTTP 200 or 302 redirect to portal home
Response contains a session cookie (e.g. session_id)
Session cookie has Secure and HttpOnly flags set
trace: R-HLT-023: patient shall log in using SSO
tests.executable:
import pytest
from playwright.sync_api import sync_playwright

def test_valid_sso_login_establishes_session(base_url, valid_sso_assertion, reset_rate_limit):
    with sync_playwright() as pw:
        browser = pw.chromium.launch(headless=True)
        ctx = browser.new_context()
        page = ctx.new_page()

        page.goto(f"{base_url}/portal/login")
        <helper_fn: inject_sso_assertion>(page, valid_sso_assertion)
        page.click("[data-testid='sso-login-btn']")
        page.wait_for_url(f"{base_url}/portal/home", timeout=10_000)

        cookies = ctx.cookies()
        session_cookies = [c for c in cookies if c["name"] == "session_id"]
        assert len(session_cookies) == 1, "session_id cookie not set"
        sc = session_cookies[0]
        assert sc["secure"], "cookie missing Secure flag"
        assert sc["httpOnly"], "cookie missing HttpOnly flag"

        browser.close()

tests.name: test_invalid_sso_assertion_is_rejected
preconditions: An expired or tampered SSO assertion is available
No prior rate-limit state for the test patient
actions: POST /portal/login with an expired/tampered SSO assertion
Observe response status and page content
expected: HTTP 401 or login page re-rendered with error message
No session_id cookie is set in the response
Patient sees a notification indicating authentication failure
trace: R-HLT-023: failed attempts shall notify the patient
R-HLT-023: error pathway – invalid SSO assertion
tests.executable:
import pytest
from playwright.sync_api import sync_playwright

def test_invalid_sso_assertion_is_rejected(base_url, invalid_sso_assertion, reset_rate_limit):
    with sync_playwright() as pw:
        browser = pw.chromium.launch(headless=True)
        ctx = browser.new_context()
        page = ctx.new_page()

        page.goto(f"{base_url}/portal/login")
        <helper_fn: inject_sso_assertion>(page, invalid_sso_assertion)
        page.click("[data-testid='sso-login-btn']")
        page.wait_for_selector("[data-testid='auth-error-msg']", timeout=8_000)

        error_text = page.inner_text("[data-testid='auth-error-msg']")
        assert error_text, "No error notification displayed to patient"

        cookies = ctx.cookies()
        session_cookies = [c for c in cookies if c["name"] == "session_id"]
        assert len(session_cookies) == 0, "session_id must not be set on failure"

        browser.close()

tests.name: test_rate_limit_triggered_at_threshold
preconditions: Rate-limit threshold N is known and configurable (default 5 attempts)
Rate-limit counter for test patient is reset to zero
actions: Submit N-1 failed login attempts with invalid SSO assertions
Verify each attempt returns 401 (not yet rate-limited)
Verify HTTP 429 is returned and patient is notified
expected: Attempts 1 through N-1 return HTTP 401 without rate-limit headers
Attempt N returns HTTP 429 with Retry-After header
Patient notification text references rate-limiting or lockout
trace: R-HLT-023: failed attempts shall be rate-limited
R-HLT-023: error pathway – 429 when too many attempts
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

RATE_LIMIT_THRESHOLD = 5

def test_rate_limit_triggered_at_threshold(base_url, invalid_sso_payload, reset_rate_limit):
    session = requests.Session()

    for attempt in range(1, RATE_LIMIT_THRESHOLD):
        resp = session.post(
            f"{base_url}/portal/login",
            json=invalid_sso_payload,
            allow_redirects=False,
        )
        assert resp.status_code == 401, (
            f"Expected 401 on attempt {attempt}, got {resp.status_code}"
        )
        assert "Retry-After" not in resp.headers, (
            f"Unexpected rate-limit on attempt {attempt}"
        )

    resp_threshold = session.post(
        f"{base_url}/portal/login",
        json=invalid_sso_payload,
        allow_redirects=False,
    )
    assert resp_threshold.status_code == 429, (
        f"Expected 429 on attempt {RATE_LIMIT_THRESHOLD}, got {resp_threshold.status_code}"
    )
    assert "Retry-After" in resp_threshold.headers, "Missing Retry-After header on 429"
    body = resp_threshold.json()
    assert body.get("message"), "Patient must receive a rate-limit notification message"

tests.name: test_session_state_transitions_on_login
preconditions: Patient is in Unauthenticated state (no active session)
Application state machine supports Unauthenticated -> Authenticating -> Authenticated
actions: Assert portal home redirects to /portal/login (Unauthenticated state)
Submit valid SSO assertion (transition to Authenticating)
Verify redirect to /portal/home and session cookie present (Authenticated state)
Assert protected endpoint /portal/profile returns 200 using the session
expected: Session cookie is active and authorises access to /portal/profile
No intermediate error states encountered during transition
trace: R-HLT-023: patient shall log in using SSO
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests
from playwright.sync_api import sync_playwright

def test_session_state_transitions_on_login(base_url, valid_sso_assertion, reset_rate_limit):
    with sync_playwright() as pw:
        browser = pw.chromium.launch(headless=True)
        ctx = browser.new_context()
        page = ctx.new_page()

        resp = page.goto(f"{base_url}/portal/home")
        assert "/portal/login" in page.url, "Unauthenticated user must be redirected to login"

        <helper_fn: inject_sso_assertion>(page, valid_sso_assertion)
        page.click("[data-testid='sso-login-btn']")
        page.wait_for_url(f"{base_url}/portal/home", timeout=10_000)
        assert "/portal/home" in page.url, "Must reach Authenticated state at /portal/home"

        cookies = {c["name"]: c["value"] for c in ctx.cookies()}
        assert "session_id" in cookies, "session_id cookie required for Authenticated state"

        api_resp = requests.get(
            f"{base_url}/portal/profile",
            cookies={"session_id": cookies["session_id"]},
        )
        assert api_resp.status_code == 200, "Authenticated session must access /portal/profile"

        browser.close()
